description_zh: Australia's IT industry continues to grow rapidly, with digital transformation driving surging demand for tech talent across all sectors. This industry is one of the most visa-friendly for international graduates, with many tech companies actively recruiting talent with overseas backgrounds.

Australia's finance industry is centred around the Big Four banks, with Sydney and Melbourne serving as major Asia-Pacific financial hubs. Large banks and financial institutions recruit significant numbers of graduates each year, with steady demand for bilingual international graduates.

Mining is a pillar of the Australian economy, with average salaries among the highest of all industries, particularly concentrated in Western Australia and Queensland. International graduates in engineering and geology are well-regarded, though most positions require working in remote areas.

Healthcare is Australia's largest employment sector, with an ageing population driving long-term talent shortages. Registered nurses are consistently on the skilled migration occupation list. International graduates need to register with AHPRA, after which job prospects are very promising.

Infrastructure development and the energy transition are driving strong demand for all types of engineers. Civil, electrical, and mechanical engineers are all on the skills shortage list. International engineering graduates need to pass the Engineers Australia competency assessment, after which migration and employment pathways are well-established.

Australia's education sector has a large workforce, with particular shortages in early childhood and secondary teaching. However, teacher registration has extremely high English requirements, and international graduates typically need IELTS 7.0 or above in all bands, creating a high entry barrier.

Major infrastructure projects and housing construction continue to drive expansion in the construction industry, with strong demand for project management and quantity surveying roles. Visa sponsorship for international graduates is relatively limited in this sector, so obtaining local certifications first is recommended.

Accounting is one of the most popular fields of study among Chinese international students, with the Big Four and mid-tier firms recruiting large numbers of graduates each year. After obtaining CPA or CA qualifications, career progression is well-defined, and accounting remains on the Medium and Long-term Strategic Skills List.

The legal industry offers high salary ceilings, but has the highest entry barrier for international graduates. There is some demand for bilingual lawyers in China-Australia cross-border commercial law and immigration law, representing a differentiated path for international graduates.

Australia is a major global agricultural exporter, and agricultural technology (AgTech) has been developing rapidly in recent years. However, overall salaries in this sector are relatively low, and most positions are in regional areas, limiting its appeal for international graduates.

International tourism has recovered strongly in the post-pandemic era, and the hospitality and tourism industry faces severe labour shortages. However, overall salaries are relatively low with irregular working hours, and employer-sponsored visa opportunities are limited.

The retail sector has a large workforce, and major retail groups offer graduate management trainee programs. Head office roles in procurement, data analysis, and supply chain are more attractive to international graduates.

The digital marketing sector is growing strongly, with rising demand for data-driven marketing and social media talent. International graduates who are familiar with the Chinese social media ecosystem have a unique advantage in helping Australian businesses expand into the Chinese market.

Management consulting encompasses Big Four consulting divisions, MBB, and boutique consulting firms. Graduate programs are highly competitive but offer higher starting salaries. Candidates with bilingual skills and Asia-Pacific market insight have a competitive edge.

The Australian government is strongly pursuing renewable energy targets, with surging investment in wind, solar, and hydrogen energy, making this the fastest-growing sector for employment. The field urgently needs engineers and project management professionals, and companies are generally willing to sponsor visas for international graduates.
